codeReview.summary: AGENTS.md and the local .dev/agent-docs/architecture.md satisfy most of the requested agent-doc infrastructure work, but the feature does not actually fulfill VAL-DOCS-005 because SKILL.md still advertises 21+ languages instead of the contract's required 60+ language count.

issues.description: VAL-DOCS-005 requires SKILL.md to reflect the expanded 60+ language support, but this line still says Vera parses '21+ languages'. The feature is marked as fulfilling VAL-DOCS-005 in features.json and the handoff, so that validation assertion is currently unmet.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker read validation-contract.md but still only verified removal of '--features local' and presence of '--local'; they never checked the VAL-DOCS-005 language-count requirement. The infra-worker / worker-base flow should explicitly require reviewing every validation assertion claimed in features.json before handoff.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md:21-23 requires SKILL.md to reflect 60+ languages; the transcript and handoff verification only list grep checks for '--features local' and '--local', while SKILL.md:5 still says '21+ languages'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance says '.factory/' is gitignored, but tracked .factory files still exist and were included in this feature commit. That makes it easy to ship unrelated shared-state edits with a feature unless staged diffs are reviewed explicitly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md says 'All .factory/, .dev/, .agents/, .tmp/ directories are gitignored'; root .gitignore includes '.factory/'; commit e623d558a05e96cdb55447a3a3ee78ebebc0f9a5 nevertheless changed .factory/library/architecture.md, .factory/library/environment.md, and .factory/library/user-testing.md.

summary: Review failed. The feature added the requested AGENTS.md and local .dev/agent-docs/ architecture doc, and README/SKILL agent-internal checks otherwise look correct, but SKILL.md still reports 21+ languages so the feature does not satisfy VAL-DOCS-005 as claimed.
